heading: Value in entry; amendment
label: 1487 Value in entry; amendment
text: The importer of record or his agent may, under such regulations as the Secretary of the Treasury may prescribe, at the time entry is made, make in the entry such additions to or deductions from the cost or value given in the invoice as, in his opinion, may raise or lower the same to the value of such merchandise.
notes: Prior ProvisionsProvisions somewhat similar to those in this section were contained in , § III, I, , which were substituted for provisions made by the Customs Administrative , , as amended by , , and as further amended by the Payne-Aldrich Tariff , . Section III of the act of 1913 was superseded by , , and was repealed by section 643 thereof. Section 487 of the 1922 act was superseded by section 487 of act , comprising this section, and repealed by section 651(a)(1) of the 1930 act.act Oct. 3, 1913, ch. 1638 Stat. 184Act of June 10, 1890, ch. 407, § 726 Stat. 134act July 24, 1897, ch. 11, § 3230 Stat. 211Act of Aug. 5, 1909, ch. 6, § 2836 Stat. 95act Sept. 21, 1922, ch. 356, title IV, § 48742 Stat. 962June 17, 1930Provisions for addition to the invoice values made by R.S. § 2900, were repealed by section 29 of the Customs Administrative Act.

Amendments1983— substituted “importer of record” for “consignee” before “or his agent”.Pub. L. 97–4461953—Act , struck out “or at any time before the invoice or the merchandise has come under the observation of the appraiser for the purpose of appraisement,” after “at the time entry is made,”.Aug. 8, 1953
